meta.title: Mythic and analog compute-in-memory MCQ bank
meta.choiceOrder: Canonical source order; the interactive quiz shuffles displayed choices.
meta.license: Original study questions. Source IDs point to the primary material used for each explanation.

questions.prompt: In an ideal current-mode analog CIM array, which relationship performs the multiplication inside one memory cell?
choices: I = V times G
V = I plus G
P = V divided by G
Q = C times frequency
questions.explanation: The input is represented by voltage V and the programmed weight by conductance G. Ohm's law gives cell current I = V times G; column-current summation supplies the accumulation.

questions.prompt: What is the primary architectural energy advantage of compute-in-memory for neural inference?
choices: It avoids repeatedly moving matrix weights to a separate arithmetic unit
It eliminates all activation movement
It makes every operation exact FP64
It removes the need for any digital logic
questions.explanation: Weights remain where the MVM occurs, reducing the dominant weight-fetch traffic. Activations, outputs, control, and unsupported operations still move and often remain digital.

questions.prompt: Why is an ADC needed after a typical analog CIM column operation?
choices: To convert the analog accumulated result into a digital code
To program the model weights during training only
To route PCIe packets
To refresh SRAM bits
questions.explanation: The array produces current, charge, voltage, or duration. The ADC digitizes that result so local digital logic can accumulate, activate, route, and continue execution.

questions.prompt: A conductance cannot be negative. What is a common way to encode signed weights?
choices: Represent a weight as the difference between positive and negative conductances
Reverse the chip clock for negative values
Store negative values only in DRAM
Ignore every negative weight
questions.explanation: Differential mapping uses G+ minus G-. Offset coding and other mappings also exist, but differential pairs are a common direct solution and cost extra cells or read paths.

questions.prompt: What is the main tradeoff of bit-serial activation input?
choices: Simpler input circuitry at the cost of additional cycles and accumulation
No converter or timing logic is required
It guarantees FP32 accuracy
It stores activations permanently
questions.explanation: Bit-serial or phased inputs reduce analog driver complexity and headroom requirements, but higher precision requires more cycles and digital shift-and-add work.

questions.prompt: Which memory technology is central to Mythic's publicly disclosed analog compute engine?
choices: Embedded flash
HBM3E
Magnetic tape
Optical disk
questions.explanation: Mythic's disclosed architecture performs analog matrix multiplication in embedded flash arrays that also retain the model weights.

questions.prompt: Which set of blocks appears beside the analog compute engine in the disclosed M1076 tile?
choices: SRAM, 16-bit SIMD, 32-bit RISC-V nano-processor, and NoC router
Only an x86 core and HBM controller
A GPU shader cluster and ray-tracing core
Only flash cells with no digital support
questions.explanation: The tile is deliberately hybrid. Local SRAM, SIMD, control, and routing handle operations and orchestration that do not belong in the analog matrix array.

questions.prompt: What does Mythic's public software workflow call its analog representation of 8-bit deployment values?
choices: ANA8
FP64X
CUDA-A
RRAM-BF16
questions.explanation: Mythic describes converting trained FP32 models into an integer 8-bit analog representation called ANA8, with retraining paths for stricter accuracy targets.

questions.prompt: Why does Mythic use a graph compiler in addition to model quantization?
choices: To map, pack, schedule, and generate code for fixed arrays and digital tile resources
To replace all weight cells with CPU instructions
To manufacture the flash devices
To eliminate every unsupported operator mathematically
questions.explanation: Quantization changes representation. Compilation must still partition matrices, allocate buffers, schedule dataflow, route activations, and generate control code.

questions.prompt: What did Mythic's 2018 Hot Chips presentation say about the input DAC in that implementation?
choices: It used digital approximation instead of a full input DAC
It used one 16-bit DAC per flash cell
It had no ADC or DAC boundary
It accepted only analog camera voltages
questions.explanation: The presentation explicitly described leaving the input DAC for a future implementation and achieving the result through digital approximation.

questions.prompt: How should Mythic's December 2025 claim of 120 TOPS/W and roughly 100x system energy advantage be classified in a research ledger?
choices: A vendor roadmap and internal benchmark claim pending public independent reproduction
A universally reproduced scientific constant
A measured result from the 2022 NeuRRAM paper
A guaranteed value for every model and batch size
questions.explanation: The announcement is useful for current direction but does not publish enough method and independent model-level evidence to treat it as a generally verified result.

questions.prompt: A matrix dimension exceeds one physical array. What must the system usually do?
choices: Partition it across arrays and combine partial results
Drop the extra rows and columns
Increase the physical array at runtime
Run only the bias vector
questions.explanation: Large projections are tiled spatially or temporally. This creates partial sums, additional routing, and scheduling work that an end-to-end cost model must include.

questions.prompt: Why can low array utilization damage real TOPS/W?
choices: Converters and peripheral circuits still consume energy while many cells do no useful work
Unused cells automatically become FP64 cores
The model gains extra accuracy for free
NoC traffic always falls to zero
questions.explanation: Peak metrics assume dense, well-utilized arrays. Irregular shapes can leave cells idle while ADC, driver, routing, and control costs remain.

questions.prompt: Which transformer operation is the strongest default candidate for analog CIM?
choices: Large dense linear projections with reused weights
Softmax normalization with dynamic exponentials
Tokenizer string parsing
Control-flow-dependent beam search bookkeeping
questions.explanation: Dense weight-stationary matrix-vector products fit analog arrays. Softmax, normalization, dynamic control, and indexing generally need flexible digital execution.

questions.prompt: What should a compiler cost model include beyond the number of MAC operations?
choices: Array utilization, ADC phases, partial sums, buffers, routing, digital fallback, and off-chip traffic
Only source-code line count
Only the model's final accuracy
Only the flash programming voltage
questions.explanation: End-to-end efficiency depends on all work surrounding MVM. Counting ideal MACs alone hides the effects that often dominate a deployed graph.

questions.prompt: What does iterative write-verify address most directly?
choices: Programming a memory cell closer to its target conductance
Routing activations between chips
Choosing a tokenizer vocabulary
Compiling C++ templates
questions.explanation: Write-verify alternates programming pulses with reads until the cell approaches its target. It reduces static weight error but costs programming time and device cycles.

questions.prompt: Which non-ideality causes a correctly programmed PCM weight to change over time?
choices: Conductance drift
Instruction-cache associativity
Tensor rank
PCIe lane reversal
questions.explanation: PCM resistance or conductance drifts as its physical state relaxes. Drift-aware training and recalibration can limit model-level impact but do not remove the underlying physics.

questions.prompt: Why does increasing ADC resolution not guarantee the same increase in effective MVM precision?
choices: Device variation, array noise, parasitics, and input error may already dominate
An ADC cannot output digital values
Higher resolution always decreases signal range to zero
Weights stop being non-volatile
questions.explanation: The effective number of bits is limited by the entire analog signal chain. A finer quantizer cannot recover information already lost to noise, distortion, or programming error.

questions.prompt: Why is analog in-memory training generally harder on device endurance than inference?
choices: Training updates weights repeatedly, whereas inference can keep weights mostly static
Inference always writes every weight for every sample
Training never uses matrix operations
Inference requires perfectly symmetric updates
questions.explanation: Static inference programs weights infrequently. Training performs many incremental updates, exposing endurance, update linearity, asymmetry, and high-precision accumulation problems.

questions.prompt: Which mitigation most directly addresses large activation or weight outliers consuming analog dynamic range?
choices: Channel scaling, clipping, outlier splitting, or selective mixed precision
Increasing package pin count only
Disabling every residual connection
Replacing the NoC with UART
questions.explanation: Outlier-aware scaling and mixed precision keep typical values above the noise floor while routing sensitive values through a larger range or more precise path.

questions.prompt: What software baseline did NeuRRAM use for its software-comparable accuracy claim?
choices: Software models quantized to 4-bit weights
An unlimited FP64 supercomputer with no quantization
A random untrained model
Only a binary neural network
questions.explanation: NeuRRAM reported fully hardware-measured results comparable to software models quantized to 4-bit weights. Naming the baseline is essential when interpreting parity.

questions.prompt: In the 2023 IBM RNNT demonstration, how did the best reported hardware WER compare with the FP software baseline?
choices: 9.258% hardware WER versus 7.452% FP baseline
0% hardware WER versus 100% FP baseline
Both were exactly 1.000%
The paper reported no software baseline
questions.explanation: The five-chip result with weight expansion reached 9.258% WER, 1.81 percentage points above the 7.452% FP baseline and within the paper's software-equivalent threshold.

questions.prompt: What did weight expansion do in the 2023 RNNT study?
choices: Used extra rows and digital preprocessing to increase signal-to-noise ratio for a sensitive matrix
Compressed every weight into one unreliable bit
Removed all LSTM layers
Converted speech into images
questions.explanation: The method factored a sensitive matrix through an expanded representation. Signal grew faster than independent noise, but the method used more rows and digital preprocessing.

questions.prompt: Why does the 2025 mixed memristor/SRAM result not prove that pure analog accuracy is solved?
choices: Its central method sends sensitive layers or kernels to SRAM CIM or digital precision
It contains no memory at all
It evaluates only sorting algorithms
It forbids mixed precision
questions.explanation: The less-than-0.45% degradation result is impressive precisely because the architecture is heterogeneous. Selective precise execution is part of the solution.

questions.prompt: Which part of the 2025 ALBERT study remained outside the analog MVM hardware path?
choices: Attention computation and several activation, pooling, and classifier operations ran in software
All fully connected weights
Every model parameter
The seven GLUE tasks themselves
questions.explanation: More than 99% of model weights were represented in hardware, but attention and nonlinear/vector operations were implemented in software, so the demonstration was not end to end.

questions.prompt: What problem does the 2026 fault-free matrix representation solve most directly?
choices: Static stuck-device faults through a product of two adjustable submatrices
Every source of temporal random noise without overhead
All transformer software compilation
The physical endurance of every memory technology
questions.explanation: Adaptive matrix factorization can route around many faulty devices and achieved very high DFT matrix similarity. It does not eliminate every noise, drift, converter, or system issue.

questions.prompt: What is the most defensible conclusion from the combined Nature-family evidence through July 2026?
choices: Software-comparable accuracy is real for scoped workloads with co-design, but no universal analog accuracy solution has been demonstrated
Analog CIM can never run any useful neural network
Every analog chip is now exact for every model
Only simulation results exist
questions.explanation: Measured chips have crossed important accuracy and scale thresholds. The results remain conditional on workload, baseline, calibration, retraining, redundancy, mixed precision, and system boundaries.

questions.prompt: Why can gradient descent-based programming intentionally leave a PCM cell farther from its nominal target conductance?
choices: It minimizes the measured array-level MVM error, so a conductance offset can compensate small peripheral nonlinearities
Nominal weights are never used by a neural network
A farther conductance always consumes zero energy
It converts every analog operation into FP64 software
questions.explanation: The programming objective is functional MVM accuracy rather than independent cell accuracy. The measured array can therefore implement the target operator better with physical values that compensate part of its static nonlinearity.

questions.prompt: What is the main lesson of the 2026 GPT-2 AIMC sensitivity preprint for compiler granularity?
choices: Profile and map individual transformer projections because sensitivity can differ sharply inside the same decoder block
Always place every first and last block in analog
Map each individual scalar weight through a separate NoC
Treat all projections as equally sensitive
questions.explanation: The modeled profile found a highly sensitive internal attention-output projection while many middle-block projections were tolerant. Projection-level mapping is finer than block-level placement without requiring weight-level routing.

questions.prompt: When evaluating an analog-CIM accuracy claim in an interview, what should you ask first?
choices: Was it measured on fabricated silicon, simulated, or inferred from device traces?
What color was the evaluation board?
How many slides were in the presentation?
Did the model name contain a vowel?
questions.explanation: Evidence type controls how strongly a result can be generalized. Full-chip measurement includes non-idealities that device-trace simulation often misses.

questions.prompt: What is the best first benchmark plan for a new AIMC accelerator?
choices: Define workload and baseline, measure end-to-end accuracy, latency, energy, utilization, corners, and drift, then report mitigation cost
Report ideal array TOPS only
Use one hand-selected sample
Ignore digital and communication energy
questions.explanation: A credible plan fixes the model, dataset, baseline, system boundary, operating conditions, and time horizon before comparing efficiency and accuracy.

questions.prompt: A chip is accurate at room temperature immediately after calibration but fails after a week at high temperature. What is the best next investigation?
choices: Separate temperature-dependent gain/offset from device drift using references, repeated measurements, and selective recalibration
Increase the marketing TOPS number
Delete all validation data
Assume the compiler changed itself
questions.explanation: The experiment should isolate reversible PVT effects from time-dependent state drift, then test whether reference-based calibration or reprogramming restores accuracy.
